lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Always humans running through Burntmeat's kitchen these days. It never used to be like dis.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, don't get any funny ideas! You promised you'd never cook another human if I helped My Arm grow goutweed.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: An' dat promise I is keeping! Never another human shall Burntmeat cook. Human taste plenty good raw if you add enough goutweed.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Anyway, Burntmeat been hoping to see you again. Got important visitor here, an' we need your help.

lines.speaker: Wolfbone
lines.text: It good to meet you, [player name]. Burntmeat tells me you very helpful human.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: So, you ready for another quest, [player name]? You get good reward if you help us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will Burntmeat reward me with burnt meat again? I've been here before, you know!

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Silly human pretends [he/she/they] not liking lovely burnt meat! But okay, Burntmeat promises not to give you more burnt meat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll take your quest.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Good human! You not regret dis, Burntmeat is sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So tell me about this quest.

lines.speaker: Wolfbone
lines.text: I come from troll town called Weiss, up in cold mountains to the north. We heard of trolls here, an' wanted to meet.

lines.speaker: Wolfbone
lines.text: Our chief, Mother, want to trade. Weiss trolls invented stuff maybe these trolls want. An' maybe these trolls invented stuff we want in Weiss.

lines.speaker: Wolfbone
lines.text: So Mother want these trolls to send ambassador up to Weiss, maybe teach us their inventions, an' in return I teach Weiss inventions here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're planning on sending someone to Weiss to teach them the technology invented by the Stronghold trolls?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: That correct! An' Burntmeat decided to send My Arm. He best troll to go to Weiss and teach stuff there. He already training assistant to take over goutweed farming.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why in the heck would you choose My Arm?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: My Arm is good choice! He been to distant lands, met strange people, come back with new learnings for Stronghold. He obviously best troll for dis job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Wolfbone come from Weiss over icy mountains, but you not go that route. It too hard for Stronghold trolls. We not practiced climbing icy mountains.

lines.speaker: Wolfbone
lines.text: That correct. You find boat and go to Weiss by sea instead. Wolfbone tell you the way...

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Okieday, now you go fetch My Arm an' tell him where he got to go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm very excited 'bout going to Weiss to teach stuff to da trolls. Better hope dey like hearing 'bout goutweed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Meet me at Larry's boat. I helped him rather a lot with those penguins, so hopefully he'll let us borrow it. It's west of the mountains, near Rellekka.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm know da place, it on coast near smelly fur- wearing human town you call Rellekka. See you in a bit.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Hey, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm doing another quest for Burntmeat.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat nice. Burntmeat always cook lovely burnt meat for you when you help us before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, he did. Lots of burnt meat filling my inventory. That was Burntmeat's reward.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: What Burntmeat need from you dis time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's a troll town called Weiss, up in the north. They want a troll from the Stronghold to go and teach them about the technology you've invented here.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: 'An Burntmeat choose My Arm to go? Dat sound like great honour! It make good sense, too - My Arm got all da goutweed knowledge.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Stronghold trolls not really invented much else. We know bashing things with rocks make 'em dead, but maybe Weiss trolls know dat already.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: But dat not a problem, [player name]. My Arm know lots 'bout growing goutweed. It gonna be fine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you mind leaving your home?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Eh... My Arm not mind. My Arm like seeing places, like dat jungle village where da nice people give us goutweed stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Those nice people might have given you anything you liked, if it would make you stop damaging their village.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Anyway, My Arm not mind going to Weiss. No family here now, not since My Other Arm died. Got apprentice ready to take over goutweed farming. Dis gonna be fine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So My Arm very happy to go teach goutweed farming to trolls in Weiss. It so exciting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't there anything else you can teach them?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Stronghold trolls not really invented much else. We know bashing things with rocks make 'em dead, but maybe Weiss trolls know dat already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wolfbone said we should go by sea. Apparently the mountains are too hard to cross, so he told me how to get there by sea.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm been on boat before, an' not like it. But My Arm not like crossing cold mountain either... eh, we better go by boat. You got a boat ready?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I once helped a guy called Larry with some penguins; he's got a boat near here, west of the mountains, near Rellekka. Come and meet me there, and we'll try borrowing it.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Okieday, My Arm know how to find it. It on coast near smelly fur-wearing human town you call Rellekka. See you in a bit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be off.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm look forward to getting on with da job when you ready!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I can't speak right now. I have too much to do. I will contact you when the time is right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I transport My Arm in your boat?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Well, yes, I'm sure you've transported your arm every time you've used my boat. Isn't it always attached to the rest of your body?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My Arm is a troll.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Your arm is... a troll? It's really not. I can see both of them. Sorry, have you had your brain shrunk into a penguin suit too many times?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This kind of thing happens a lot when I take My Arm out to places.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You take your arm out to places? And what the heck is THAT?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind, I just need to borrow your boat.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Sorry, I distinctly heard you say you wanted to transport your arm in it. Have you had your brain shrunk into a penguin suit too many times? I don't lend my boat to mad people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This kind of thing happens a lot when I take My Arm out to places.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You take your arm out to places? And what the heck is THAT?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm here!

sections.title: Talking to Larry after My Arm arrives

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I can't speak right now. I have too much to do. I will contact you when the time is right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I transport My Arm in your boat?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: That thing is an arm?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat 'thing' is a troll, an' My Arm not like being called a thing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is My Arm, yes. We'd like to borrow your boat, so we can visit more trolls in the north. My Arm is going to teach them to farm.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm is a farmer, yup! Got a farm.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Your arm's arm has a farm... Please make this stop!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Eh, [player name], My Arm thinks dis human kinda dumb. Can we use boat or not? My Arm getting bored.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You want my boat? Yes, yes, take my boat and go a very long way from me! And take your arm with you!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Are we dere yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Are we dere yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You going too slow. Let My Arm drive!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... well, let's see how this goes...

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Is we going backwards?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm didn't know boats could go backwards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I should drive again.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You gettin' angry with My Arm again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dis boat smell of bird poo.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We didn't have many choices.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: So... are we dere yet?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm not gonna be able to climb dat. Maybe you look for other way first?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm not gonna be able to get through dat hole. It got all rocks in da way. You wanna clear it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't see whether it's safe to clear the blockage from out here, or whether that would collapse the cave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe if I got into the cave from above, I could clear it more safely from inside.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Well, we here. My Arm not enjoyed boat ride. Weiss must be up top of cliff, but My Arm not gonna be able to climb up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wolfbone said we should moor here and go up the cliffs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He might have assumed you're good at climbing, since you actually live on a mountain!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyway, I'll have a look around.

sections.title: Upon climbing up all the rocks

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A cave! Maybe I can go down there, and find a way to mine through the blocked passage from the inside, to let My Arm in...

sections.title: Crossing the fence before talking to Boulder

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm supposed to be introducing My Arm to this town as their new friend. Maybe it'd be worth talking to the inhabitants before assuming I need to break into their home.

lines.speaker: Boulder
lines.text: What you want, human? You not coming in. Weiss is troll town - humans not welcome.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My Arm has come to make friends with you.

lines.speaker: Boulder
lines.text: Boulder not sure exactly what you offering, human, but it not sound like something Boulder want to happen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But My Arm's come a very long way for this, and he's very excited about what he can teach you.

lines.speaker: Boulder
lines.text: Boulder still not sure what you offering, human, but you definitely not doing it in here. Go away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wolfbone's sent My Arm from the Troll Stronghold.

lines.speaker: Boulder
lines.text: Human arm might taste good, maybe, so if Wolfbone sent your arm as snack, we happy to take it an' eat it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not letting you eat my arm.

lines.speaker: Boulder
lines.text: Urgh - human arm attached to human body with human mouth what keep talking an' talking. Shut up an' go away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You only need to let My Arm in, not me.

lines.speaker: Boulder
lines.text: Human sound confused about how body work. See, mortal body is intrinsic part of mortal being. It not like we got our souls floating up in da sky controlling bodies like puppets.

lines.speaker: Boulder
lines.text: We mortal beings - troll, human, goat - we are our bodies, and our bodies are us. You human got dumb ideas 'bout mind/body dualism.

lines.speaker: Boulder
lines.text: Anyway, Boulder not here to talk ontological distinction between mind an' matter, especially with dumb human who not know what his arm is. So push off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back!

lines.speaker: Boulder
lines.text: Maybe next time you come, Boulder get ear-plugs.

sections.title: Descending hole after evading all the trolls

lines.speaker: Squirrel
lines.text: Human intruder!

lines.speaker: Icicle
lines.text: Got ya!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a good thing I got in here before those trolls arrived!

sections.title: Upon walking close enough to the cave exit

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, there's the cave exit I need to clear. Then My Arm can get into this cave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Weiss trolls won't trust me until they can see I've really brought a troll here. But they won't be able to see My Arm unless he walks far enough into the cave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The water looks shallow at the north end. Maybe it can be filled up with rocks, or something, so My Arm could walk further into the cave...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and those trolls seem to have plenty of rocks to throw into the water...

sections.title: Upon creating a long enough stepping stone path

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I reckon those stepping stones will let My Arm enter the cave far enough for the trolls to see him! I'd better clear that obstruction so he can get in.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: T'anks, [player name].

lines.speaker: Pebble
lines.text: Stop chuckin' rocks - it a troll! Bring him to Mother!

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Welcome to Weiss, troll. I am Mother. Mother sorry about your trouble getting in here.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: We not trust humans. Also, your human keep talking about its arm, and we not want mad human in our town. But now Mother knows human is your servant, we let it come and go freely.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: T'anks, Mother. [player name] is helpful human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not his servant.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Humans are not welcome in Weiss. If you are not the servant of this troll, you should go away and never return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you called Mother, Mother?

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: A troll child is named after the first thing they try to eat. This is tradition of troll people. My full name is Mother's Left One.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: * cough *

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's move on with the chat.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: This here is Don't Know What, loyal bodyguard and chief sentry. He like a son to Mother.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: This old troll is Odd Mushroom. He is big clever-clogs, and it makes him talk funny, but Mother puts up with it. Odd Mushroom invents stuff for Mother.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: It's a real pleasure to meet you, My Arm. And you too, [player name]. I look forward tremendously to working with you both.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: And here come Mother's daughter, Snowflake.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Hello Father.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Oh... [player name]... look!

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Hello, visitor. I am Snowflake.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm very pleased to see Snowflake.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Your arm is pleased to see me?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm got named My Arm by my dad, after My Arm tried to nibble his arm... sorry, My Arm not want to bore you.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: You are not boring - I like how My Arm talks. My father is Mother. He named me after a snowflake.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat a pretty name. For a pretty troll. Maybe you get to know My Arm better soon?

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: I would like to spend more time with My Arm, yes.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Snowflake! We men got business to talk! Shut up and get out.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Sorry, Father.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Aww...

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: You really shouldn't speak to her that way, Mother. It's incredibly rude.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: You shut up too, or Mother break your clever-clogs head open an' see how smart your brain is when it outside your skull!

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Snowflake got to respect boss, an' that is Mother. When Mother retire as boss, Don't Know What will marry Snowflake. Then he will be boss, an' Snowflake got to respect him.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: She doesn't want that, you know.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: This your last warning - shut up! Weiss needs strong boss - that is how we survive. For now, Mother is strongest boss. Some day, Don't Know What will be strongest boss. Otherwise trolls die out.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: So, arm troll, you come from Stronghold to share what you know. What you got?

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: In return, we can teach you all about the special fires we've invented. They can be very useful.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Well, My Arm know 'bout goutweed farming.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: That sounds jolly exciting!

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Farming? That a stupid skill for a man-troll. Proper man-troll should use his muscles. You teach us something better than that, or you get out and not come back!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Uh... [player name], what do we do now? If he kick us out, My Arm never gonna see Snowflake again. Help!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell him goutweed is delicious.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yeah! Mother should try goutweed. It taste so good! Everything taste better with goutweed, an' My Arm know how to grow it.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Mother is not interested in eating stupid vegetables. Mother likes wolf flesh, freshly killed, with blood running down Mother's jaw.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Mother gets real warm fuzzy feeling inside, eating furry wolf.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: You better have something else to offer, or you go home an' stop wasting Mother's time.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell him how tough Stronghold trolls are.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yeah, we really good at combat. Trolls near Stronghold, dey chuck rocks really hard. An' My Arm helped [player name] save da goutweed patch from big flappy bird thing!

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Mother has trolls already who chuck rocks really hard. Also Mother has seen birds - they are not scary.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dis bird - it were really big though...

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Mother is not impressed. Have Stronghold trolls nothing else to offer?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: What we do now, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell him to show respect to his daughter.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yeah, My Arm not like how Mother talks to Snowflake.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Mother is boss; Snowflake will obey her father. Arm troll is making Mother angry. Now, what you teaching us?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dis not working, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell him you love his daughter!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Really? If My Arm start talking' bout what My Arm want to do with his daughter, he probably gonna get angry or freaked out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I meant on a more emotional level...

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Mother can hear you! It not matter. Mother's daughter, Snowflake, will marry Don't Know What. Then he can be boss when Mother retires.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Trolls need strong boss. Mother kept trolls alive through Mother's strength! Otherwise trolls would have died long ago. Once Mother retires, Don't Know What is strongest.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: So Don't Know What will marry Snowflake and be boss of Weiss. No more talk on this! You arm troll, you teach us something good or go home!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get back to you in a bit.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Sorry, Mother, My Arm got nothing else. But My Arm not want to leave - can My Arm stay please?

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Arm troll is useless. Get out of here!

sections.title: Talking to My Arm, Snowflake, or Odd Mushroom
Talking to any of them again

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Have you asked the Wise Old Man to help us? We only need him to pretend to be dead, so My Arm can claim credit for killing him, and impress Father.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Yes, that would impress Mother very much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've not discussed it with him yet.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Please ask him, [player name]! For me and My Arm!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Oh, [player name], My Arm is being sent home. Stupid big Mother not interested in goutweed. My Arm would prefer to stay with Snowflake.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: I wish My Arm did not have to leave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mother wasn't very nice to Snowflake in there.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: My father, Mother, did not like me talking to My Arm. Whoever marries me will rule Weiss after Father retires or dies, and Father wishes that to be Don't Know What, the biggest sentry.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm not happy 'bout dat.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: My Arm is not as big as Don't Know What, and I think he is less strong too. Father would not approve of him as a suitor for me.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Mother should mind own business, an' not tell Snowflake who to marry.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Father is... horrible to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Snowflake, you seemed to like My Arm.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: He is different from other man-trolls. I know already that I want My Arm by my side.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm want that too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Odd Mushroom, why are you here with us?

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Oh, Mother's been annoying me for years. He doesn't care about anything except fighting. Whatever I invent for him, he ignores it completely if he can't use it in combat.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: That's why he dismissed My Arm's farming expertise too. He's really holding Weiss back. Trolls should aspire to be more than just lumbering monsters.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Mother won't change himself, and none of us are strong enough to force him, especially with Don't Know What guarding him... but maybe My Arm can help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sounds like you want My Arm to overthrow Mother for you.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: I deplore violence. No, I want Mother to let Snowflake marry My Arm, instead of marrying Don't Know What. Snowflake's smart, and she'll rule us better.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: But if she marries Don't Know What, he'll insist on ruling Weiss himself like Mother does - and he's no better than Mother. So I'd rather My Arm stayed here and married Snowflake.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: The choice should be from my heart, not from anyone else - though I like this plan very much!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm like plan too. My Arm marry Snowflake, an' she become boss of town, an' My Arm teach town to farm goutweed. Yup, good plan.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: So we will need Mother to gain respect for My Arm, so that he'll consent to the marriage.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: How can we get Father to respect My Arm? He likes Don't Know What because Don't Know What is strong, but My Arm is... different.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I kill Mother? That usually solves problems in quests.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: I would prefer you not to kill Father. He is cruel, but he is my blood.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Let's not have any unnecessary killing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can My Arm fight Mother?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: He really big though.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: I do not think that would be wise, dear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can My Arm fight Don't Know What?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: He look dumb, but he kinda big.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: He is dumb, but he is big. I think this would not work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does Mother respect anything except fighting?

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Mother's strength saved this tribe, in the past. That's how he became chief. He truly believes that if our chief is not physically strong, we will die out.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: You see, we have not always lived in Weiss. We used to live further south, near the dwarf town they call Keldagrim, until we were driven out...

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: That Wise Young Man drove us out into the barren icy mountains. Without Mother's strength to overcome obstacles and catch food, I believe we would have perished.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you say the dwarves called that human the 'Wise Young Man'?

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Yes, that's right. They treated him as a great hero after he drove us out of our tunnels.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know that Wise Young Man you mentioned! I did a quest with him where we saved a fishing colony. He's the Wise Old Man now.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: You know the mortal enemy of our people! What did you do in the fishing colony with him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We killed a lot of trolls.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Very well. That Wise Young Man killed many of our people, and I am disappointed that you worked with him, but I shall overlook it for My Arm.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: So if you all hate dis old man, how about My Arm kill him? Then Mother gonna really like My Arm! Old human sounds easy to kill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did some fishing and fetched some stuff.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Very well. That Wise Young Man killed many of our people, and I am glad that you are different from him.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: So if you all hate dis old man, how about My Arm kill him? Then Mother gonna really like My Arm! Old human sounds easy to kill.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Yes! If you kill the human who drove us out from the Keldagrim tunnels, Mother will call you a hero and be pleased for you to marry Snowflake. Can you go and do it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, My Arm can't kill the Wise Old Man! The Wise Old Man is far too powerful.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat a shame. But he know you, [player name], an' you helped him in fishy colony place. Maybe you ask him a favour now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're suggesting I ask him to let you kill him, just so that you can marry Snowflake?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Please!

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Ah, no. He surely won't agree to die. But perhaps he can pretend he's dead, while My Arm tricks Mother into believing it. Can you ask him, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is a very strange request!

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Please ask him, [player name]! For me and My Arm!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back in a moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Snowflake can leave with us. Then she and My Arm can be together, in the Troll Stronghold.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Um... thank you for the offer, but...

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Ah. Mother is our chief, and as his daughter, Snowflake is the heir to the throne of Weiss. So Snowflake or her future husband will rule this town. My Arm may be a charming chappy...

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: T'anks, mushroom troll.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: ... but Snowflake shouldn't be expected to run away from Weiss, abandoning what's hers. No, we should try to keep My Arm here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back in a moment.

sections.title: Talking to the Wise Old Man
Talking to the Wise Old Man again

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player name].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I asked you to get me a cadava potion from the Varrock Apothecary, calibrated to suit my age, and to build me a mahogany coffin. How are you getting on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've not built the coffin yet.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: So I see. Make sure it's mahogany when you do - I wouldn't be seen dead in a cheap oak or teak one. You can build it here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've not got the potion from the Apothecary yet.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Really? He's in Varrock, near the south-west corner of the city. He's a funny chap, but he knows his trade.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player name].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: It's good of you to pop in, [player name]. What brings you to Draynor today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My Arm needs your help.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You should see a doctor about your arm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not my arm, I mean My Arm.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: ... you should see a doctor about your brain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not helping.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you pretend you're dead?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That looked very strange.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You asked me to pretend I'm dead. That's what I imagine it would be like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me explain... A troll called My Arm needs to pretend he's killed you. This will impress his girlfriend's father, Mother.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: His girlfriend's father, Mother?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. You drove Mother's tribe of trolls out of the dwarf tunnels near Keldagrim, long ago, and Mother's hated you ever since.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I remember driving trolls out of those tunnels, yes. So now there's a troll called My Arm who wants to pretend he's killed me in revenge, to impress his girlfriend's father, who is called Mother?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. If Mother thinks My Arm has killed you, he'll let My Arm marry his daughter, Snowflake.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Hmmmph. That's quite a big favour you're asking. I've spent much of my life saving people from monsters like that, not providing family mediation services for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll get rewarded if you help.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I don't think so, thank you. In my experience, a troll's idea of a quest reward is probably something useless like a pile of burnt meat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You owe me a favour after the Fishing Colony quest.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I suppose you did save my life from the Sea Troll Queen. That has to count for something...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Very well, I'll help. I'll need a cadava potion from the Varrock Apothecary, calibrated to suit my age. Also build me a proper mahogany coffin here - I wouldn't be seen dead in a cheap oak or teak one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I'll try to get the potion and build you a coffin here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll think about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I am the Apothecary. I brew potions. Do you need anything specific?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you remember the Cadava potion you made for Juliet? Can you make another one for the Wise Old Man? He said you'd need to calibrate the dosage to suit his age.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I suppose he'd need a smaller dose than Juliet did, yes. She's a healthy young woman in the prime of her life, while he's been around for decades.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Well, I'm sure you remember the drill. I'll supply the wing of rat, tail of frog, ear of snake and horn of dog... and you need to bring me some Cadava berries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah, cadava berries. I'll be back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, I brought some of those.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: That's very clever. I hope you've got a real feeling of pride and satisfaction from that.

sections.title: Talking to the Wise Old Man after all is prepared

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player name].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I asked you to get me a cadava potion from the Varrock Apothecary, calibrated to suit my age, and to build me a mahogany coffin. How are you getting on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I've built the coffin.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Maybe I should have told you to make a gilded one, with a marble pedestal and crystal ornamentation. It would look more fitting... but no, this will do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I got the potion from the Apothecary.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Good. Hand it to me, then let us proceed with this charade.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, that looks about right. I wouldn't want to drink more than this, or I might risk an overdose. Well, here goes...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: * BURP *

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Wise Old Man has disguised himself as a corpse in a coffin to help you two.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: He very obliging, dis mortal enemy of Weiss. Must have been big favour he owed [player name]. Okieday, give coffin to My Arm, an' My Arm give him to Mother.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Okieday, now My Arm has Wise Old Man in box! We go show Mother now. Maybe he like My Arm for killing big enemy of trolls.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Here, Mother, look what My Arm done! My Arm done killed your old enemy! He kick you out of Keldagrim long back, now My Arm got him dead in gift-box!

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: My Arm did this? My Arm killed him?

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: My Arm is clearly a great warrior, beyond the skills of any Weiss troll. We should welcome him into the tribe.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yeah, let My Arm stay in Weiss please. Also, can My Arm marry your daughter?

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Huh. Mother must think about that. But look at this body here...

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: What kind of fight was this? Where is the blood? When Mother kills an enemy, there is blood. Much blood. Blood, brains, gut juices.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: This old man could have died of old age in his sleep. How is that revenge for what he did to Mother? And to Mother's people?

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Come on, Mother. That man was our mortal enemy, and now My Arm's brought us his corpse. Accept My Arm as a friend and let's move on like civilised people.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: You shut up, Odd Mushroom! Always talking about moving on, talking about civilising, talking about progress - what kind of troll are you?

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: You messed up Mother's daughter - she uses long words like you, talks about leading trolls into future.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: She needs to marry proper troll, like Don't Know What, and remember how trolls are. Don't Know What can lead tribe for real.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Stop it, Father! You cannot tell me how to think, or how to live, or how to love. And I will be with My Arm.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: I am Mother. I am your father, and you will obey me! This Arm troll brings nothing but trouble, and Mother does not trust him. In fact...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: * snore *

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Um... Don't worry, Mother, this is totally normal for a dead wizard. It's the magic escaping from the cadaver...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: * parp *

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't remember Juliet doing that!

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: A TRICK!

sections.title: Talking to the Wise Old Man

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Those salt formations are really quite remarkable. Your troll friends are having a private moment, so I'll study these while my head recovers from that potion.

sections.title: Talking to My Arm, Snowflake, or Odd Mushroom

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Snowflake, are you there? Reason tells me there is light in here, but my mind sees darkness.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Snowflake?

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: I am here. Father did not hurt me much. Can you stand, Odd Mushroom?

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: ... no, I don't think so. I am glad you are here with me, Snowflake. I must tell you something...

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: I can smell that Mother has lit a Fire of Domination, up above. He never cared for most of my inventions, but he liked that one.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: That's the one that traps his enemies, and helps him beat them to a pulp. Definitely his favourite. He must intend to kill you all.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Fire of Domination?

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: ... I suppose I have no secrets, now, even from the age- old enemy of Weiss. But you and I are thinkers, and in other circumstances we might have been friends.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: These salts in the mine - I invented ways to combine them, unlock their power. In a fire, they can work wonders.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: The Fire of Domination will block teleportation magicks, and it also aids Mother in a fight. It makes him incredibly dangerous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So we can't teleport out of here?

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: That hole leads down to the sea. But it's far too narrow for a troll to get through.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm not want to enter smelly hole!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I don't think I could get in there either, even if the smell weren't abhorrent. But [player name] might be able to fit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll never leave you.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You good friend, [player name].

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Anyway, Odd Mushroom was telling us about the fires he'd invented.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: I wrote down my inventions, but many of my notes are lost. Mother only ever cared about the Fire of Domination, and fires he could use for combat.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: It can be hard to interest trolls in the delicate skill of Firemaking, sometimes. But perhaps Snowflake can find my lost notes, and carry on the science. She's far smarter than her father.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: But I never invented a fire that could stave off the eternal dark, and... I feel it coming.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Snowflake, you must take over Weiss. You saw what Mother did to you, and to me, and to My Arm. Trolls will never be more than thuggish monsters as long as he rules.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Mother did hit My Arm pretty hard.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: I know what I must do. With My Arm at my side, I will avenge you. You were always more of a father to me than Mother ever could be.

lines.speaker: Odd Mushroom
lines.text: Thank you, child...

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That'll be handy if I feel like escaping.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Aww.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Anyway, Odd Mushroom was telling us about the fires he'd invented.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: My Arm, [player name], Old Man, we are going to get out of here, and punish my father for what he has done.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: You have my support.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: And My Arm.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I hate to be indelicate, but... it may not be possible to overthrow Mother without killing him, especially if the fire makes him as dangerous as Odd Mushroom said.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Father killed Odd Mushroom. Odd Mushroom was like a father to me. Mother must pay the price. Also I think he really will kill us all.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: [player name], you heard Odd Mushroom's warning. We can't use our teleport magicks, so the only escape - for you - is down the smelly hole.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Also, if we drop anything in here, I bet you're not going to be able to retrieve it. Items dropped in here will be lost forever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I can't teleport, and I may lose stuff? Okay.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: [player name] is brave human an' good friend for My Arm and Snowflake!

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Old Man, do your magic and open that door!

lines.speaker: Squirrel
lines.text: Aargh - they're out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not ready to fight yet.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Okieday, we hang around here until you are.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: ... so you two kill the stupid Arm troll and the human it brought with it. Mother will sort Snowflake out permanently.

lines.speaker: Odd Stone
lines.text: Mother cannot mean to kill Snowflake, surely?

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: You want to do it yourself?

lines.speaker: Odd Stone
lines.text: Um... that was not what I meant.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Question Mother again, and it will be your blood that Mother drinks instead. Snowflake betrayed her father, and Mother does not forgive.

lines.speaker: Butterfly
lines.text: We are not happy about this.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Mother does not need you to be happy. Mother requires only that you obey, or die. We are trolls, not softy liberals like Snowflake.

lines.speaker: Squirrel
lines.text: OLD MAN IN THE DUNGEON!

lines.speaker: Squirrel
lines.text: OLD MAN IN THE DUNGEON! Thought you ought to know.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Of course he is in the dungeon. Mother put him there.

lines.speaker: Squirrel
lines.text: No, I mean he got out.

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: Aaargh!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, that was jolly charming, but I'm off now. Good bye.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Sure would have been easier gettin' up to dis place if My Arm could do tellypot like dat!

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: [player name], we owe you so much, and we will thank you - but we still need one small favour first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You need... one small favour...? I've heard that said before.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yup, you good at dis stuff. My Arm been telling Snowflake how you fetched all kinda stuff to Stronghold for My Arm before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... yes, as an experienced adventurer, I have indeed fetched stuff for people.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Good. Now please fetch us some goat dung. You should find some in the goat pen outside. Meanwhile I need to assign some of my trolls to different duties around Weiss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you want with goat dung?

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: I think it will be useful to us. You will see why soon enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back.

sections.title: Attempting to pick up goat dung without a bucket

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is the kind of thing that's best carried in buckets.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Have you got the goat dung?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I've not got any goat dung yet.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: You should find some in the goat pen outside. Meanwhile I need to assign some of my trolls to different duties around Weiss.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: My Arm, what do you think, dear?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm t'inks dis gonna work, yeah!

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Good! My Arm said you needed camel dung to grow goutweed. But goat dung is similar enough - I will make my trolls build farming patches from goat dung instead.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: She got trolls to use goat poo to make two herb patches! One for us to grow goutweed, an' one for you, 'cos you been good friend. We protect your stuff from disease.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: I have searched through Odd Mushroom's possessions. Sadly much of his research has been lost. I have copied what I could find into a book for you.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: He would have wanted you to have it - some of his inventions are not useful here, but you are a traveller and may find places to use them.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You give it looksie now, an' see if you learn good stuff! Go on, Snowflake work hard on dis for you!

sections.title: Talking to Snowflake before reading Odd Mushroom's notes

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You read book from Snowflake yet? She work hard on it for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I've got it here.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Oh.

sections.title: Talking to Snowflake after reading Odd Mushroom's notes

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I read that book you gave me.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Good! Now you know all Odd Mushroom's fire learnings, an' maybe they be useful to you.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: When we have finished here, I would like you to try the Fire of Nourishment. Although Boulder made two herb patches as we told him, crops will not grow without such a fire to strengthen them.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Herbie patches are outside Weiss, through gap in fence, west of town. One patch is for you, an' Boulder gonna protect it from disease for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll bear it in mind. Through the gap in the fence, to the west of the town, you say?

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: That is right, [player name]. You can mine the salts in the cave below, next to the prison.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Now, let us thank you for bringing us together, for saving our lives and for marrying us...

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: We got him!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm sorry 'bout your dad, Snowflake. But he kinda gonna kill us, anyway.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: I am sorry too. Father was flawed, but he did much for our people, and he should have retired with honour long ago.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Old Man, [player name], thank you both for your help.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I'm not sure this was my proudest adventure, but you're welcome. I think I'll head off home to Draynor quite soon.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Very well. You will always be welcome here. Now, that is enough of the past. I feel the future beginning in Weiss... Let us summon my tribe.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Trolls of Weiss, I am Snowflake, daughter of Mother, my father. Mother is dead, and I claim the throne.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yup, My Arm helped Snowflake kill him.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Let's not emphasise that bit now, eh?

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Does any troll here challenge me?

lines.speaker: Odd Stone
lines.text: Nope, we like Snowflake.

lines.speaker: Goat Poo
lines.text: Snowflake nicer than Mother. We okay with Snowflake.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Then the throne of Weiss is mine. I also call you to witness our marriage. [player name], please step forward.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: I, Snowflake, give myself to My Arm. I take My Arm as my support and comforter. I will hold My Arm by my side for life.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: My Arm is my friend forever. I will share the sorrows of life with My Arm, and I will share the joys of love with My Arm.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm will support Snowflake. My Arm will be at Snowflake's side. My Arm takes Snowflake by the hand for life.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm will be Snowflake's companion when sun shines an' life is bright. My Arm will be Snowflake's comfort in dark places.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I pronounce you husband and wife!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Congratulations!

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: We now perform the ancient dance of love...
